statement: An order can be pending, paid, shipped, or cancelled
An order can be archived by a nightly archive job that references the archived status directly

verdict.reason: ARCHIVED has zero live rows; the archive job was retired in 2019 and never ran in production; the comment is false

statement: A customer placed an order, which set it to pending
A customer paid for a pending order, which set it to paid
An operator shipped a paid order, which set it to shipped
An order is placed (then pending), then paid, then shipped
A shipped order cannot be cancelled

evidence.source: docs/ops-runbook.md (support must reset to pending before cancelling)

statement: A support agent reset a shipped order back to pending via a manual support action

evidence.source: docs/ops-runbook.md (support reset flow, restricted to shipped, a few times a week)

statement: Resetting a high-value order pauses with a sleep to avoid racing the legacy invoice sync

verdict.reason: the sleep is a 2014 workaround for a race condition in a legacy sync job; it is implementation noise, not domain behaviour

statement: A refund amount is rounded up to the next cent, favoring the customer on half-cent boundaries

verdict.reason: Math.ceil produces customer-favoring rounding on half-cent boundaries; banker's rounding was specified; the test pins the bug, not the intent

statement: An invoice is a posted billing document issued to accounting

verdict.reason: the entity is a quotation; the name invoice is a historical artefact from a pre-2013 rebrand; nothing posts to accounting

statement: A quotation can be draft, sent, accepted, or expired
The order archive job was retired in 2019 and never ran in production

evidence.source: data/order_status_counts.csv (no archived row; zero live archived orders)

verdict.reason: resolves the contradiction with CLM-0002: the archive job was retired in 2019 and never ran; retired-job history is not carried-forward domain value

statement: The entity named invoice is a customer quotation; nothing is posted to accounting

evidence.source: docs/billing.md (Nothing is posted to accounting; pre-2013 rebrand note)

verdict.reason: billing.md, model fields (quoted_total, valid_until), and UI strings (quote.*) all corroborate this entity is a customer quotation; no accounting integration exists

statement: Users call a shipment a parcel

evidence.source: ui/strings.json (order.status.shipped Parcel dispatched, order.actions.track Track your parcel)

verdict.reason: UI strings consistently use parcel as the user-facing term for a shipment across all shipping-related copy
